title: Human Resource Manager Job at Kiyix Recruitment Hub
content:
Kiyix  Recruitment Hub is a recruitment agaency that bridges the gap between employers and emplyoyees. Our goal at Kiyix Recruitment and Motivation Hub is to help create one million jobs in Nigeria by 2030. We strive to help make businesses in Africa great by providing them with good staff and enablingthe staff to become great. 

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager
Job Location: Mushin, Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly experienced and proactive Human Resources Officer to join our team in the FMCG Food sector.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for supporting and implementing HR initiatives, ensuring compliance with company policies, and contributing to the overall effectiveness of the Human Resources function.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage recruitment, onboarding, and employee induction processes.
	Maintain employee records and HR databases.
	Monitor staff attendance, leave administration, and disciplinary procedures.
	Performance management and employee development initiatives.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, company policies, and HR best practices.
	Handle employee relations matters and resolve workplace issues professionally.
	Prepare HR reports and documentation as required.
	Payroll administration and staff welfare activities.
	Coordinate training and development programs.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Human Resources, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of proven HR experience
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict-resolution skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HR management systems.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional HR certification will be an added advantage.


Salary
N250,000 - N400,000 per month.  

Compensation& Benefits

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Wisdom Kwati Smart City - We are a company dedicated to building sustainable cities by integrating smart technologies in our service delivery, thereby providing our clients with luxurious yet tremendously affordable real estate solutions as well as investment opportunities and property&nbsp;&nbsp;management.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Head Accountant

Job Location: Katampe District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Chartered Accountant will be responsible for overseeing the company&#39;s financial operations, ensuring accurate financial reporting, budgeting, tax compliance, internal controls, and statutory compliance.
	The successful candidate will provide strategic financial guidance to support business growth, maintain financial integrity, and ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards and regulatory requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports.
	Manage budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes.
	Oversee accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, and cash flow management.
	Ensure compliance with IFRS, tax laws, and statutory regulations.
	Coordinate internal and external audits and implement audit recommendations.
	Prepare and file statutory returns, including VAT, PAYE, WHT, and Company Income Tax.
	Maintain strong internal controls and financial risk management processes.
	Monitor financial performance and provide strategic recommendations to management.
	Supervise finance staff and ensure effective financial operations.
	Liaise with regulatory agencies, auditors, banks, and other stakeholders.


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Master&#39;s Degree or MBA is an added advantage.


Professional Certifications:


	Mandatory: ICAN or ACCA.
	Additional certifications in Finance, Taxation, Risk Management, or IFRS are an added advantage.


Other Requirements:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax laws and financial regulations.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and integrity.


Required Skills:


	Financial Reporting (IFRS)
	Budgeting &amp; Forecasting
	Financial Analysis
	Tax Planning &amp; Compliance
	Audit &amp; Internal Controls
	Cash Flow &amp; Treasury Management
	Microsoft Excel &amp; Accounting Software (Sage, SAP, QuickBooks, Dynamics, etc.)
	Leadership and Team Management
	Analytical &amp; Problem-Solving Skills
	High Integrity and Attention to Detail.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@wisdomkwatismartcity.com using &quot;Chartered Accountant&quot; as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",

Head Accountant Job at Wisdom Kwati Smart City




        
    

        
        Wisdom Kwati Smart City - We are a company dedicated to building sustainable cities by integrating smart technologies in our service delivery, thereby providing our clients with luxurious yet tremendously affordable real estate solutions as well as investment opportunities and property  management. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Head Accountant

Job Location: Katampe District, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Chartered Accountant will be responsible for overseeing the company's financial operations, ensuring accurate financial reporting, budgeting, tax compliance, internal controls, and statutory compliance.
	The successful candidate will provide strategic financial guidance to support business growth, maintain financial integrity, and ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards and regulatory requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports.
	Manage budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes.
	Oversee accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, and cash flow management.
	Ensure compliance with IFRS, tax laws, and statutory regulations.
	Coordinate internal and external audits and implement audit recommendations.
	Prepare and file statutory returns, including VAT, PAYE, WHT, and Company Income Tax.
	Maintain strong internal controls and financial risk management processes.
	Monitor financial performance and provide strategic recommendations to management.
	Supervise finance staff and ensure effective financial operations.
	Liaise with regulatory agencies, auditors, banks, and other stakeholders.


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Master's Degree or MBA is an added advantage.


Professional Certifications:


	Mandatory: ICAN or ACCA.
	Additional certifications in Finance, Taxation, Risk Management, or IFRS are an added advantage.


Other Requirements:


	Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax laws and financial regulations.
	Excellent communication and report-writing skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and integrity.


Required Skills:


	Financial Reporting (IFRS)
	Budgeting & Forecasting
	Financial Analysis
	Tax Planning & Compliance
	Audit & Internal Controls
	Cash Flow & Treasury Management
	Microsoft Excel & Accounting Software (Sage, SAP, QuickBooks, Dynamics, etc.)
	Leadership and Team Management
	Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills
	High Integrity and Attention to Detail.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Chartered Accountant" as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Wisdom Kwati Smart City - We are a company dedicated to building sustainable cities by integrating smart technologies in our service delivery, thereby providing our clients with luxurious yet tremendously affordable real estate solutions as well as investment opportunities and property&nbsp;&nbsp;management.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Product Manager

Job Location: Katampe District, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Product Manager will oversee the development, positioning, pricing, profitability, and market growth of Kwati Nigeria Ltd&#39;s sugar product portfolio.
	The role is responsible for identifying market opportunities, managing product lifecycles, driving product innovation, and ensuring products meet customer needs while achieving business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement product strategies for sugar products.
	Manage the product lifecycle from development to market launch.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis.
	Develop pricing and product positioning strategies.
	Monitor product performance, sales, and profitability.
	Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, Production, Quality Control, and Supply Chain teams.
	Drive product improvements, packaging innovations, and new product development.
	Ensure compliance with food safety and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare product performance reports and recommendations.


Educational Qualification


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Economics, Food Science, Agribusiness, Industrial Management, or a related field.
	MBA or Master&#39;s Degree is an added advantage.


Experience:


	6&ndash;10 years of experience in Product Management, Brand Management, Category Management, or Commercial Management.
	Experience in FMCG, food manufacturing, sugar, beverages, or consumer goods industries is preferred.
	Proven track record of successfully managing and growing product portfolios.


Professional Certifications (Preferred):


	Certified Product Manager (CPM)
	Project Management Professional (PMP)
	Chartered Institute of Marketing (CIM)
	Nigerian Institute of Marketing of Nigeria (NIMN)
	PRINCE2 Practitioner
	Certified Supply Chain Professional (CSCP)


Other Requirements:


	Strong understanding of the Nigerian FMCG market.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and reporting tools.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects.
	Willingness to travel nationwide when required.
	High level of integrity, innovation, and result orientation.
	Strong commercial and business acumen.


Required Skills:


	Product Management
	Market Research &amp; Analysis
	Product Pricing &amp; Profitability Management
	Brand &amp; Portfolio Management
	Data Analysis and Reporting
	Strategic Thinking
	Commercial Acumen
	Project Management
	Leadership and Stakeholder Management
	Strong Communication and Negotiation Skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should forward their CV to: careers@kwati.ng using &quot;Product Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",

Product Manager Job at Wisdom Kwati Smart City




        
    

        
        Wisdom Kwati Smart City - We are a company dedicated to building sustainable cities by integrating smart technologies in our service delivery, thereby providing our clients with luxurious yet tremendously affordable real estate solutions as well as investment opportunities and property  management. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Product Manager

Job Location: Katampe District, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Product Manager will oversee the development, positioning, pricing, profitability, and market growth of Kwati Nigeria Ltd's sugar product portfolio.
	The role is responsible for identifying market opportunities, managing product lifecycles, driving product innovation, and ensuring products meet customer needs while achieving business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement product strategies for sugar products.
	Manage the product lifecycle from development to market launch.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis.
	Develop pricing and product positioning strategies.
	Monitor product performance, sales, and profitability.
	Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, Production, Quality Control, and Supply Chain teams.
	Drive product improvements, packaging innovations, and new product development.
	Ensure compliance with food safety and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare product performance reports and recommendations.


Educational Qualification


	Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Economics, Food Science, Agribusiness, Industrial Management, or a related field.
	MBA or Master's Degree is an added advantage.


Experience:


	6–10 years of experience in Product Management, Brand Management, Category Management, or Commercial Management.
	Experience in FMCG, food manufacturing, sugar, beverages, or consumer goods industries is preferred.
	Proven track record of successfully managing and growing product portfolios.


Professional Certifications (Preferred):


	Certified Product Manager (CPM)
	Project Management Professional (PMP)
	Chartered Institute of Marketing (CIM)
	Nigerian Institute of Marketing of Nigeria (NIMN)
	PRINCE2 Practitioner
	Certified Supply Chain Professional (CSCP)


Other Requirements:


	Strong understanding of the Nigerian FMCG market.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and reporting tools.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects.
	Willingness to travel nationwide when required.
	High level of integrity, innovation, and result orientation.
	Strong commercial and business acumen.


Required Skills:


	Product Management
	Market Research & Analysis
	Product Pricing & Profitability Management
	Brand & Portfolio Management
	Data Analysis and Reporting
	Strategic Thinking
	Commercial Acumen
	Project Management
	Leadership and Stakeholder Management
	Strong Communication and Negotiation Skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified applicants should forward their CV to: [email protected] using "Product Manager" as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Officer",
  "description": "Tonia Pharmacy Limited was incorporated in the year 2003 (RC: 497235) to carry on business as a&nbsp;&nbsp;pharmaceutical company. Since inception, the company has grown to become one of the reputable&nbsp;&nbsp;pharmacies in Nigeria, with several branches located within the FCT Abuja.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Inventory / Supply Chain
Reports To: Inventory Manager / Head of Inventory

Job Summary


	The Inventory Officer is responsible for maintaining accurate inventory records, monitoring stock levels, receiving and issuing products, conducting stock counts, ensuring compliance with inventory control procedures, and supporting the efficient distribution of pharmaceutical and healthcare products across all company locations.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and verify incoming pharmaceutical products and other inventory items against purchase orders and delivery notes.
	Ensure all received products meet quality standards, including checking batch numbers, expiry dates, quantities, and packaging integrity.
	Accurately record all inventory transactions in the inventory management system.
	Monitor stock levels and promptly notify management of low stock, overstock, slow-moving, or expired products.
	Prepare and process stock requisitions, transfers, and issues to branches and departments.
	Maintain accurate inventory records and documentation.
	Conduct daily, weekly, monthly, and annual stock counts and reconcile any discrepancies.
	Investigate inventory variances and prepare reports with recommendations.
	Ensure proper storage of products in accordance with company SOPs and Good Storage Practices (GSP).
	Monitor product expiry dates and coordinate the removal or return of near-expiry and expired items.
	Maintain proper labeling, shelving, and organization of inventory.
	Assist in stock audits conducted by internal and external auditors.
	Prepare periodic inventory reports for management.
	Ensure compliance with company inventory policies and regulatory requirements.
	Coordinate with procurement, warehouse, logistics, and branch personnel to ensure efficient stock movement.
	Maintain confidentiality and security of inventory records.
	Ensure warehouse cleanliness, safety, and compliance with health and safety regulations.
	Report damaged, missing, or obsolete inventory promptly.
	Participate in system updates, inventory optimization initiatives, and process improvement activities.
	Perform any other duties assigned by Management.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Inventory accuracy rate.
	Stock variance percentage.
	Timeliness of stock reconciliation.
	Percentage of expired products.
	Order fulfillment accuracy.
	Stock availability rate.
	Timely submission of inventory reports.
	Compliance with inventory SOPs.
	Audit performance.
	Reduction in inventory losses and wastage.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Pharmacy, Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Accounting, or any related field.
	2&ndash;3 years of inventory management experience, preferably in a pharmacy, hospital, warehouse, or healthcare environment.
	Professional certification in Inventory Management or Supply Chain Management is an added advantage.


Required Skills and Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of inventory management principles.
	Excellent numerical and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management software.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good organizational and time management skills.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and under pressure.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	High level of integrity and accountability.
	Ability to maintain confidential information.
	Knowledge of FEFO (First Expiry, First Out) and FIFO inventory principles.
	Understanding of pharmaceutical storage requirements and Good Storage Practices.


Working Conditions:


	Monday to Saturday or as scheduled by Management.
	May be required to work weekends, public holidays, or overtime during stock counts and audits.
	Work may involve standing for extended periods and lifting inventory within approved safety limits.
	Regular travel between company branches and warehouse locations may be required.


Expected Behaviour:


	Demonstrate professionalism and integrity at all times.
	Maintain confidentiality of company information.
	Promote teamwork and collaboration across departments.
	Adhere strictly to company policies, SOPs, and regulatory requirements.
	Deliver excellent internal customer service to all branches and departments.
	Uphold the values and reputation of Tonia Pharmacy Ltd.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: hr@toniapharmacy.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-18",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-25",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Tonia Pharmacy Limited"
    
  },

Inventory Officer Job at Tonia Pharmacy Limited




        
    

        
        Tonia Pharmacy Limited was incorporated in the year 2003 (RC: 497235) to carry on business as a  pharmaceutical company. Since inception, the company has grown to become one of the reputable  pharmacies in Nigeria, with several branches located within the FCT Abuja. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Inventory / Supply Chain
Reports To: Inventory Manager / Head of Inventory

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Inventory Officer is responsible for maintaining accurate inventory records, monitoring stock levels, receiving and issuing products, conducting stock counts, ensuring compliance with inventory control procedures, and supporting the efficient distribution of pharmaceutical and healthcare products across all company locations.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and verify incoming pharmaceutical products and other inventory items against purchase orders and delivery notes.
	Ensure all received products meet quality standards, including checking batch numbers, expiry dates, quantities, and packaging integrity.
	Accurately record all inventory transactions in the inventory management system.
	Monitor stock levels and promptly notify management of low stock, overstock, slow-moving, or expired products.
	Prepare and process stock requisitions, transfers, and issues to branches and departments.
	Maintain accurate inventory records and documentation.
	Conduct daily, weekly, monthly, and annual stock counts and reconcile any discrepancies.
	Investigate inventory variances and prepare reports with recommendations.
	Ensure proper storage of products in accordance with company SOPs and Good Storage Practices (GSP).
	Monitor product expiry dates and coordinate the removal or return of near-expiry and expired items.
	Maintain proper labeling, shelving, and organization of inventory.
	Assist in stock audits conducted by internal and external auditors.
	Prepare periodic inventory reports for management.
	Ensure compliance with company inventory policies and regulatory requirements.
	Coordinate with procurement, warehouse, logistics, and branch personnel to ensure efficient stock movement.
	Maintain confidentiality and security of inventory records.
	Ensure warehouse cleanliness, safety, and compliance with health and safety regulations.
	Report damaged, missing, or obsolete inventory promptly.
	Participate in system updates, inventory optimization initiatives, and process improvement activities.
	Perform any other duties assigned by Management.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Inventory accuracy rate.
	Stock variance percentage.
	Timeliness of stock reconciliation.
	Percentage of expired products.
	Order fulfillment accuracy.
	Stock availability rate.
	Timely submission of inventory reports.
	Compliance with inventory SOPs.
	Audit performance.
	Reduction in inventory losses and wastage.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Pharmacy, Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, Accounting, or any related field.
	2–3 years of inventory management experience, preferably in a pharmacy, hospital, warehouse, or healthcare environment.
	Professional certification in Inventory Management or Supply Chain Management is an added advantage.


Required Skills and Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of inventory management principles.
	Excellent numerical and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management software.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good organizational and time management skills.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and under pressure.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	High level of integrity and accountability.
	Ability to maintain confidential information.
	Knowledge of FEFO (First Expiry, First Out) and FIFO inventory principles.
	Understanding of pharmaceutical storage requirements and Good Storage Practices.


Working Conditions:


	Monday to Saturday or as scheduled by Management.
	May be required to work weekends, public holidays, or overtime during stock counts and audits.
	Work may involve standing for extended periods and lifting inventory within approved safety limits.
	Regular travel between company branches and warehouse locations may be required.


Expected Behaviour:


	Demonstrate professionalism and integrity at all times.
	Maintain confidentiality of company information.
	Promote teamwork and collaboration across departments.
	Adhere strictly to company policies, SOPs, and regulatory requirements.
	Deliver excellent internal customer service to all branches and departments.
	Uphold the values and reputation of Tonia Pharmacy Ltd.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply
